annotations.label: Quadratic sieve

anchor.snippet: The quadratic sieve algorithm ( QS ) is an integer factorization algorithm

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Nat.factorization` for prime factorizations of natural numbers, but I found no declaration defining the quadratic sieve algorithm or proving its correctness as a factorization algorithm.

annotations.label: QS practical speed

anchor.snippet: in practice, the second-fastest method known

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib declaration about quadratic-sieve performance or comparative practical speed among factorization methods.

annotations.label: QS under 100 digits

anchor.snippet: It is still the fastest for integers under 100 decimal digits

annotations.note: I found no declaration combining the quadratic sieve with a 100-decimal-digit threshold or a fastest-factorization claim.

annotations.label: General-purpose factorization algorithm

anchor.snippet: It is a general-purpose factorization algorithm

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes natural-number prime factorization via `Nat.factorization`, but I found no declaration classifying algorithms as general-purpose factorization algorithms.

annotations.label: General-purpose running time dependence

anchor.snippet: its running time depends solely on the size of the integer to be factored

annotations.note: `AkraBazziRecurrence` models some running-time recurrences as functions of a natural-number size parameter, but I found no factorization-specific general-purpose dependence criterion.
